mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month employment growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market demand and consumer spending capacity. Rising payroll employment over a 3-month window predicts positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days as equity investors price in stronger corporate earnings from increased consumer demand.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2018 payroll employment at 148,912 thousand persons
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth periods do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative growth periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0% and continues climbing, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This signals future earnings headwinds from higher corporate borrowing costs and reduced consumer spending. Sustained FEDFUNDS elevation above 2.0% predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets front-run earnings compression.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite elevated FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture consumer and construction demand. When both series decline simultaneously over 2 months, aggregate demand is weakening. This joint weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2018 RSAFS at 501,971 million dollars, HOUST at 1,350 thousand units
failure_condition: Joint decline periods produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect (RSAFS × HOUST) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real-side economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates below its 12-month trend, supply-side constraints are easing and inflation pressure diminishes. This deceleration predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market prices in lower future inflation and stable monetary policy.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below its 12-month average growth rate
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with moderating supply constraints
failure_condition: Deceleration periods produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a lagging indicator of labor market tightness. When UNRATE falls below 4.5%, the labor market is overheated, signaling wage inflation and future Fed tightening. This tight labor condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates earnings margin compression from wage pressures.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2018 UNRATE at 4.0%, already below 4.5% threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: UNRATE < 4.5% periods produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS level (negative real rates), the Fed is accommodative. This accommodative stance predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations expand under loose monetary conditions.
failure_condition: Accommodative periods produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term (CPI growth - FEDFUNDS) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. When quarterly GDPC1 growth decelerates to below 2.0% annualized, the economy is entering a slowdown phase. This deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2018 GDPC1 at 17,371.854 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Sub-2.0% growth quarters produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity and labor utilization. When INDPRO growth accelerates while PAYEMS growth decelerates, productivity is improving and labor costs are moderating. This productivity gain predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market prices in margin expansion.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2018 INDPRO at 107.3006, June 2018 PAYEMS at 148,912 thousand
failure_condition: Productivity improvement periods produce negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect has Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
